description: FLAG epitope tag (DYKDDDDK): an engineered 8-residue hydrophilic peptide used as an affinity/epitope tag, recognized by anti-FLAG M1/M2/M5 antibodies and N-terminally cleavable by enterokinase.

references.title: A Short Polypeptide Marker Sequence Useful for Recombinant Protein Identification and Purification

references.authors: Brizzard BL, Chubet RG, Vizard DL
references.title: Immunoaffinity purification of FLAG epitope-tagged bacterial alkaline phosphatase using a novel monoclonal antibody and peptide elution
The FLAG peptide, a versatile fusion tag for the purification of recombinant proteins
references.journal: Journal of Biochemical and Biophysical Methods (2001)

references.authors: Roosild TP, Castronovo S, Choe S
references.title: Structure of anti-FLAG M2 Fab domain and its use in the stabilization of engineered membrane proteins

references.authors: Guo XY, Gao XD, Fujita M

Identification of Amino Acid Residues Responsible for the Binding to Anti-FLAG M2 Antibody Using a Phage Display Combinatorial Peptide Library

label: FLAG
note: Engineered 8-aa FLAG epitope/affinity tag (Asp-Tyr-Lys-Asp-Asp-Asp-Asp-Lys). Recognized by anti-FLAG M1/M2/M5 monoclonal antibodies. The C-terminal Asp-Asp-Asp-Asp-Lys is an enterokinase recognition/cleavage site, enabling removal of an N-terminal tag. Source-verified to PDB 8RMO chain F at 100% identity.

provenance.sequence_source: PDB 8RMO chain F (entity 1, "FLAG-tag", synthetic polypeptide, Beugelink et al. J.Mol.Biol. 2024 doi:10.1016/j.jmb.2024.168649), 100% identity over 8 aa; canonical original reference: Hopp TP et al. Bio/Technology 6:1204-1210 (1988) doi:10.1038/nbt1088-1204

functional_claims.label: FLAG is an engineered hydrophilic 8-aa epitope/affinity tag for detection and purification of recombinant proteins

source.quote: A Short Polypeptide Marker Sequence Useful for Recombinant Protein Identification and Purification

functional_claims.label: FLAG is recognized by the anti-FLAG monoclonal antibodies M1 (Ca2+-dependent, N-terminal-specific), M2 (Ca2+-independent, position-insensitive) and M5

source.quote: The FLAG peptide, a versatile fusion tag for the purification of recombinant proteins
source.section: review of M1 Ca2+-dependent and M2/M5 peptide-competitive elution

functional_claims.label: The C-terminal DDDDK of FLAG is an enterokinase (enteropeptidase) recognition site, allowing proteolytic removal of an N-terminal FLAG tag
The minimal anti-FLAG M2 epitope is DYK(D)/DYKxxD; residues D5 and D7 are dispensable for M2 binding

source.quote: identifies D1, Y2, K3, D4, D6 as the five contact residues; FLAG adopts a 3_10 helix

functional_claims.label: In the secretory pathway, Tyr2 of FLAG can be sulfated by TPST2 (SLC35B2-dependent), reducing anti-FLAG M2 and polyclonal detection of secreted/membrane proteins

source.quote: Sulfation of a FLAG tag mediated by SLC35B2 and TPST2 affects antibody recognition
